title: SOONSUN 4-Pack Dive Filter for GoPro Hero 13 12 11 10 9 8 Black Official Waterproof Housing - Red, Light Red, Magenta, 5x Close-up Macro Filters Color Correction for Underwater Photography and Video
features: Compatibility: These dive filters are compatible with original GoPro waterproof housing case for HERO13 Black / HERO12 Black / HERO11 Black / HERO10 / HERO9 / HERO8 Black (Not fit for third party housing case for GoPro; can’t be used on camera’s lens directly)

Dive Filters: The dive filters offer color correction and helps capture vibrant colors in blue, green, and shallow waters, the professional color formulas provide flawless videos in deep diving, scuba diving, snorkeling and other underwater activities

5x Close-up Macro Filter: The 5x close-up macro filter is used to magnify scenery, flower, and tiny underwater objects that require close-up details; It helps to capture vivid and clear pictures above and underwater
High Light Transmittance: These lens filters are made of high transmission materials, which ensure sufficient light breakthrough to capture high-definition photography and videos
Package Includes: 1 x red filter (deep-diving filter), 1 x light red (snorkeling) filter, 1 x magenta filter, 1 x macro filter, 1 x drawstring bag, and 4 x tether
description: Product Description Please check compatibility before purchasing Fit for GoPro Official Housing Case Only The 3-pack underwater diving filters are only compatible with official housing case for GoPro Hero 8/9/10/11/12/13 Black. Previous page Next page 1 High Transmission 2 Inner Silicone Layer 3 Slot for Tether 4 Select Correct Filters 5 Compartmentalized Pouch Some tips of using dive filters Why should I use diving filters？ When shooting in diving, the special environment underwater can cause the color of the photos taken to be bluish or greenish. To solve this problem, photographers need to use underwater diving filters to balance the colors and improve contrast in various underwater video and photography conditions. How to choose the right diving filters? Red filter is for blue or tropical water at the depth of 12 ~ 85 feet . Light red filter is for for clear fresh water at the shallow depth of 2 ~ 15 feet. Magenta filter is for green water at depth of 8 ~ 75 feet .(e.g. the lake ) Macro filter is for magnifying & capturing underwater tiny objects Comparison of using SOONSUN filters and not using SOONSUN filters The video showcases the product in use.
